purpose: The investor's route through India's incentive stack, assembled from everything this program has verified: how an investor goes from 'which schemes apply to my sector' to registered applications, approvals and incentive claims -- using NSWS as the spine, with the scheme catalog for applicability, the registry for window status, per-scheme portals and ministry connects for execution, and the repo's seven-gate lifecycle for the statutory path. Includes the candor layer an investor brochure never carries: disbursal reality by scheme grade, attrition precedents, and which official pages to distrust.
method: Synthesized from the site-verified ministry incentive catalog (16 ministries + NSWS, 2026-07-20), the scheme registry (windows/closures), the PLI report card (disbursal grades), and the project workflow schema (seven statutory gates). Portal URLs and contacts are as verified on government sites; NSWS flow verified in-session on nsws.gov.in.

workflow_steps.name: Locate your sector's incentive set
workflow_steps.what: Match your sector to the applicable instruments: production-linked (mostly closed windows -- MPMS/ECMS are the live electronics lanes), capex subsidies (SPECS-era closed; DoP SPI/SMDI, UNNATI, state packages open), park infrastructure (BHAVYA, PM MITRA, pharma/med-device parks -- units apply to SPVs), VGF (gasification, offshore wind, UDAN), credit/guarantees (CGTMSE, ECLGS 5.0, exporter guarantees under EPM), duty instruments (RoDTEP/RoSCTL/EPCG/SEZ; SPMEPCI for EVs), and price-support (EBP/SATAT offtake).
workflow_steps.tool: Ministry incentive catalog + master sector scorecard
workflow_steps.investor_note: The five-year register shows the state now pays for INPUTS (land, credit, risk, capex) more than output -- if you missed a PLI window, the live money is in capex/cluster/credit lanes.

workflow_steps.name: Check the window before anything else
workflow_steps.what: Verify the application window is actually open -- on the SCHEME PORTAL, not the ministry page (pages are provably stale; the E-DRIVE 'closure' that turned out to be an extension is the standing example). Open as of 20-Jul-2026: UNNATI (to 30-Sep-2026), Coal Gasification 2026 Round-1, DoP API-CF + SMDI calls, MoFPI cold-chain/CEFPPC/OG, ECMS (extended), ALMM enlistment, AHIDF/NLM loans, Steel R&D, NTTM rolling.
workflow_steps.tool: Scheme registry + catalog OPEN flags; NSWS gateway notices (it carried the only formal IFLDP closure notice)
workflow_steps.investor_note: PLI windows are almost all closed; successors (MPMS) and later rounds (Steel 1.2-style) are the re-entry points -- track Cabinet approvals, not ministry pages.

workflow_steps.name: Structure the entity and FDI route
workflow_steps.what: Foreign investors: confirm sector FDI cap/route (automatic vs government approval via FIFP); if any investor in the chain touches a land-border country, the amended Press Note 3 Beneficial-Owner test (Cabinet, 10-Mar-2026, PRID 2237806) applies to the whole structure -- now with defined BO criteria. Semicon 2.0 explicitly admits OCI-owned companies in the design pillar.
workflow_steps.tool: DPIIT FDI policy + PN3 amendment; this repo's FDI-sourcing country strategies
workflow_steps.investor_note: State-wise FDI data credits your registered office, not your factory -- expect your investment to 'appear' in the wrong state in official statistics.

workflow_steps.name: Enter through NSWS
workflow_steps.what: Register at nsws.gov.in (DPIIT/Invest India; 32 ministries + 34 States/UTs live) -> run the Know Your Approvals (KYA) questionnaire to generate your personalized central + state approval checklist -> apply for approvals through the single window -> use the six scheme gateways (Vehicle Scrapping, Ethanol, IFLDP, Solar PLI, Green Hydrogen, PM GatiShakti) where your scheme has one: each carries validated documents, nodal contacts and an apply flow.
workflow_steps.tool: nsws.gov.in -- Central Approvals, State Approvals, Government Schemes, KYA
workflow_steps.investor_note: NSWS helpline 1800-102-5841 (Mon-Sat 9-6); contactus-nsws[at]investindia[dot]org[dot]in; weekday 3-4pm technical grievance connect. IEM Part A (industrial entrepreneur memorandum) for manufacturing is filed via the G2B/NSWS stack -- this is gate 2 of the lifecycle and the basis on which implementation is later measured.

workflow_steps.name: Apply on the scheme's own portal
workflow_steps.what: Most incentive schemes run dedicated portals with Project Management Agencies: electronics ECMS ecms.meity.gov.in and MPMS (window TBN); IT-HW 2.pliithw.com; telecom pli-telecom.udyamimitra.in; White Goods pliwhitegoods.ifciltd.com; UNNATI unnati.dpiit.gov.in; BHAVYA nicdc.in/bhavya; textiles pli.texmin.gov.in; food PLISFPI plimofpi.ifciltd.com + SAMPADA component portals; livestock AHIDF ahidf.udyamimitra.in and NLM nlm.udyamimitra.in; solar via SECI/IREDA tenders; hydrogen SIGHT via SECI; gasification via Coal Ministry RFPs; startups startupindia.gov.in; exports via DGFT e-platform (RoDTEP/EPCG/EPM).
workflow_steps.tool: Portal per scheme (full list in the ministry catalog)
workflow_steps.investor_note: PMA contacts are the real ministry connect for PLI-family schemes: IFCI (LSEM/IT-HW/White Goods/Food: pli[at]ifciltd[dot]com, pliithw[at]ifciltd[dot]com), C-DAC (DLI), SECI/IREDA (solar/hydrogen), NICDC (BHAVYA/corridors), state SPVs (PM MITRA, drug/device parks), Empowered Committees (MAI).

workflow_steps.name: Clear the statutory gates in parallel
workflow_steps.what: The repo's seven-gate lifecycle is your critical path: IEM Part A (intent) -> land (state SPV / BHAVYA / PM MITRA plug-and-play parks compress this) -> Environmental Clearance via PARIVESH (parivesh.nic.in -- the EC register this repo showed correlates 0.96 with actual implementation; industrial ECs cluster in Gujarat 31%) -> build -> IEM Part B at commercial production (the filing that makes your investment 'real' in official statistics) -> incentive claims begin.
workflow_steps.tool: PARIVESH portal; state single windows (linked from NSWS State Approvals)
workflow_steps.investor_note: Choose states with demonstrated conversion: Gujarat runs the full chain at 33.8% of national implementation with a 178% intent-to-implementation ratio; the EC pipeline depth (ToR stage) is the best forward indicator this program found.

workflow_steps.name: Claim -- with calibrated expectations
workflow_steps.what: Claims run annually against incremental sales (PLI), on commissioning+1y (solar), per-contract (SBFAS ~14%), per-transaction (UDAN VGF), or per-loan (subventions). Calibrate to the report card: Pharma (A) and Electronics (A-) actually pay; Telecom/Food/MedDev pay modestly; Auto is accelerating from a low base; Steel/Textiles pay thinly; ACC has paid zero in five years. Programme-wide, ~15% of PLI outlay had been disbursed after five years.
workflow_steps.tool: PLI report card grades + scheme registry closure/attrition record
workflow_steps.investor_note: Precedents that matter to underwriting: 14 of 58 steel projects withdrew; White Goods quietly went 85->80 beneficiaries; an ACC awardee was removed; Drones PLI simply expired. Model the incentive as upside, not base case -- except where the instrument pays on investment (capex/VGF) rather than output.

workflow_steps.name: Monitor the policy surface
workflow_steps.what: Windows move (ECMS extended; PLI Textiles amended thrice in a year; E-DRIVE extended segment-wise), successors arrive via Cabinet (MPMS, Semicon 2.0 on one day), and new lanes open quarterly (~Rs 1.9 lakh cr of 2026 non-PLI instruments). Track the Cabinet register -- this repo's PIB index makes it a SQL query -- and re-verify your scheme's portal each quarter.
workflow_steps.tool: pib_index.sqlite (--update quarterly); this repo's scheme registry refresh
workflow_steps.investor_note: The E20 decision point (31-Oct-2026), ALMM List-II window (31-Dec-2026), UNNATI close (30-Sep-2026) and Food Processing sunset (FY27) are the nearest dated triggers on the calendar.

ministry_connects.for: Single-window registration, KYA, approval routing, scheme gateways
PLI LSEM/MPMS-era claims, IT Hardware, White Goods, Food Processing
Telecom PLI, TTDF grants, testing-cost reimbursement

ministry_connects.contact: IREDA cmd[at]ireda[dot]in +91-11-24682206-19; MNRE 011-24360707 (both via NSWS solar gateway)
ministry_connects.for: Solar PLI tranches, SIGHT hydrogen, offshore wind VGF, ALMM
Pharma PLIs, SPI/RPTUAS/API-CF, SMDI, PRIP, park SPVs

ministry_connects.contact: Via PM MITRA (7 states), Bulk Drug Parks (GJ/HP/AP), MedDev Parks (HP/MP/TN/UP), BHAVYA (NICDC)
ministry_connects.for: Plug-and-play land + state incentive layering (states top up central schemes 40-50% in semiconductor cases)

ministry_connects.contact: dgft.gov.in/CP - Niryat Bandhu mentoring; regional authorities
ministry_connects.for: IEC, RoDTEP/RoSCTL scrips, EPCG/AA, Export Promotion Mission

candor_for_investors: Three-numbers discipline: FDI inflow, committed investment and disbursed incentive are different numbers -- never let a pitch conflate them (this repo's core editorial rule).

Verify on portals, not pages: six ministry scheme pages were provably stale in the 2026-07-20 sweep; two ministry domains are dead; one ministry's scheme list doesn't render. NSWS notices and scheme portals are the reliable surfaces.

Applicant transparency is bimodal: some schemes publish beneficiary lists (DoP), many publish nothing (Bulk Drugs/Solar/ACC beneficiaries have never been named in PIB). Expect limited comparables for underwriting.

The state layer is real money: states top up central capex schemes (e.g. 40-50% additions on semiconductor subsidies) -- the state single windows hang off NSWS State Approvals; hub choice materially changes the package.

Where incentives pay on OUTPUT, model slow: ~15% of PLI outlay disbursed in five years, with grade-A exceptions (Pharma, Electronics). Where they pay on INVESTMENT (capex/VGF/parks/credit), cash arrives with the build.
